title: The Monster Monologues - Episode Two - Paint and Chat
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t01TItG_UAQ

Daniel (@dungeondive) turns Monster Monologues episode two into a paint-and-chat after his Kingdom Death night was cancelled by a sick friend. By request, he demonstrates the simple, fast "stone statue" technique on three of the narrative survivors: grey primer, a soft-tone wash, a dark-tone wash, an ash-grey drybrush, then an anti-shine finish — stressing he's an amateur, not a master, and that "perfection is the enemy of creativity." A wrinkle emerges live: the brush-on primer he's now using runs bluer and darker than his spray, and he worries it'll eat the soft browns that gave the first batch their stony look.

Between coats, he rambles engagingly. He vents about Kickstarter's "all-in culture" — the competitive bragging over who spends most, fastest, and the $10,000 gaming tables — noting his own play tables cost $50 and $100 on Craigslist, and that most backers never see a tenth of what they pledge for. He reveals he's written and recorded over 40 albums since the mid-90s (ambient and experimental work on Bandcamp), feeling the itch to return after a break. He's excited for two upcoming Kickstarters: Awaken Realms' Tainted Grail and Succubus Publishing's Middara.

----

Where do you land on Kickstarter "all-in" culture — guilty pleasure, harmless hype, or a genuine problem for the hobby?
